notes.content: Between the Fall 2025 and Fall 2027 application cycles, the School of Social Sciences, Humanities and Arts will not require transfer students to complete major specific selection criteria to be admitted to this major*. Students wanting to enroll in this major will be admitted using UC Merced's general transfer requirements**. * This only applies to certain SSHA majors. ** The General Transfer Requirements are as f ol lows: Units and GPA Complete 60 semester (90 quarter) units of UC-transferable college credit Earn a minimum grade point average of 2.40 (nonresident: 2.80) in UC-transferable courses Take no more than 14 semester (21 quarter) units as Pass/Not Pass Course Requirements/Subject Requirements Complete the following seven-course pattern, earning a grade of C- or better in each course: Two transferable English composition courses One transferable mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning course that has a published prerequisite of intermediate algebra Four transferable courses chosen from at least two of the following areas: Arts and Humanities, Behavioral and Social Sciences, Biological and Physical Sciences While major courses are not required for admissions, it is highly recommended to complete as many of the recommended major preparatory courses as possible before transferring to ensure timely progress to degree. Any major courses not completed prior to enrollment at UC Merced must be completed upon enrollment at UC Merced to ensure time to degree and graduation. Recommended, but not Required Prior to Transfer: At least two UC-Transferrable Courses in History Students are strongly encouraged to complete two courses that have been approved as equivalents to specific UC Merced courses, including at least one world history course, as this will prepare for upper division courses at UC Merced. These courses may also fulfill the Additional courses listed below. Complete one (1) Non-United States History Course : CRES 070 or HIST 005 or HIST 008 or HIST 010 or HIST 011 or HIST 030B or HIST 040 or HIST 041 or HIST 042 or HIST 051 or HIST 060 or HIST 070 or HIST 071 Complete Three (3) Additional History Courses : HIST 005 or HIST 008 or HIST 010 or HIST 011 or HIST 016 or HIST 017 or HIST 020 or HIST 021 or HIST 027 or HIST 030B or HIST 035 or HIST 039 or HIST 040 or HIST 041 or HIST 042 or HIST 043 or HIST 051 or HIST 052 or HIST 055 or HIST 055A or HIST 060 or HIST 070 or HIST 071 Courses in the major must be taken for a letter grade and may not be taken on a pass/no pass basis unless the course is only offered on a pass/no pass basis. AP Exam Score & Course Exemptions AP History: European History: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 030B AP History: US History: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 016 and HIST 017 AP History: World History: Modern: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 010 or HIST 011 UC Merced Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) credit policies are detailed in the link below: A dvance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examinations Please read the catalog page for more information about the History major. For more questions about admissions, please email: transfer@ucmerced.edu .
notes.contentHtml:
Between the Fall 2025 and Fall 2027 application cycles, the School of Social Sciences, Humanities and Arts

will not require transfer students to complete major specific selection criteria to be admitted to this major*.

Students wanting to enroll in this major will be admitted using UC Merced's general transfer requirements**.


* This only applies to certain SSHA majors.

** The General Transfer Requirements are as follows:


Units and GPA

  • Complete 60 semester (90 quarter) units of UC-transferable college credit
  • Earn a minimum grade point average of 2.40 (nonresident: 2.80) in UC-transferable courses
  • Take no more than 14 semester (21 quarter) units as Pass/Not Pass


Course Requirements/Subject Requirements

Complete the following seven-course pattern, earning a grade of C- or better in each course:

  • Two transferable English composition courses
  • One transferable mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning course that has a published prerequisite of intermediate algebra
  • Four transferable courses chosen from at least two of the following areas: Arts and Humanities, Behavioral and Social Sciences, Biological and Physical Sciences


While major courses are not required for admissions, it is highly recommended to complete as many of the recommended major preparatory courses as possible before transferring to ensure timely progress to degree. Any major courses not completed prior to enrollment at UC Merced must be completed upon enrollment at UC Merced to ensure time to degree and graduation.

Recommended, but not Required Prior to Transfer:


  • At least two UC-Transferrable Courses in History
  1. Students are strongly encouraged to complete two courses that have been approved as equivalents to specific UC Merced courses, including at least one world history course, as this will prepare for upper division courses at UC Merced.
  2. These courses may also fulfill the Additional courses listed below.


  • Complete one (1) Non-United States History Course: CRES 070 or HIST 005 or HIST 008 or HIST 010 or HIST 011 or HIST 030B or HIST 040 or HIST 041 or HIST 042 or HIST 051 or HIST 060 or HIST 070 or HIST 071


  • Complete Three (3) Additional History Courses: HIST 005 or HIST 008 or HIST 010 or HIST 011 or HIST 016 or HIST 017 or HIST 020 or HIST 021 or HIST 027 or HIST 030B or HIST 035 or HIST 039 or HIST 040 or HIST 041 or HIST 042 or HIST 043 or HIST 051 or HIST 052 or HIST 055 or HIST 055A or HIST 060 or HIST 070 or HIST 071


Courses in the major must be taken for a letter grade and may not be taken on a pass/no pass basis unless the course is only offered on a pass/no pass basis.


AP Exam Score & Course Exemptions

  • AP History: European History: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 030B
  • AP History: US History: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 016 and HIST 017
  • AP History: World History: Modern: score of 4 or 5 exempts HIST 010 or HIST 011


UC Merced Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) credit policies are detailed in the link below:

Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examinations


Please read the catalog page for more information about the History major.


For more questions about admissions, please email: transfer@ucmerced.edu.

notes.content: History majors are encouraged (but not required) to exceed the General Education language requirement. Advanced language skills are necessary for those interested in pursuing graduate training in History. History Majors may exchange up to 8 units of advanced language classes (at UC Merced or approved courses offered elsewhere including in summer language institutes) for their Additional History Courses Requirement with consent of Chair.
